name: empty_arch_satisfies_inv
reason: An architecture with zero layers vacuously satisfies inv, allowing a degenerate page table that cannot map any address space

name: unaligned_base_vaddr_well_formed
code:
proof fn phi_2_unaligned_base_vaddr_well_formed(arch: Arch, entries: Seq<NodeEntry>)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        arch.layers.len() > 0,
        entries.len() == arch.num_entries(0),
    ensures
        (Directory { entries: entries, layer: 0, base_vaddr: 1, arch: arch }).well_formed(),
{
}
reason: A directory with base_vaddr=1 (unaligned to any page boundary) passes well_formed because the alignment check is commented out in the source

name: all_invalid_entries_satisfy_inv
code:
proof fn phi_3_all_invalid_entries_satisfy_inv(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.well_formed(),
        forall|i: nat| i < d.entries.len() ==> d.entries[i as int] is Invalid,
    ensures
        d.inv(),
{
}
reason: A directory where every entry is Invalid vacuously satisfies all inv sub-conditions, meaning the spec permits a page table that maps nothing at all

name: update_wrong_page_size_preserves_inv
code:
proof fn phi_4_update_wrong_page_size_preserves_inv(d: Directory, n: nat, pte: PTE)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        n < d.num_entries(),
        pte.frame.size != d.entry_size(),
    ensures
        d.update(n, NodeEntry::Page(pte)).inv(),
{
}
reason: If a page with mismatched frame size can be inserted while preserving inv, the pages_match_entry_size invariant is ineffective

name: entry_size_equal_across_layers
reason: Two consecutive layers with identical entry_size (via num_entries=1) means a layer adds no address-space subdivision, violating the x86 expectation that each layer partitions into multiple entries
